title: Uragoga hieronymi

Uragoga smithii is a plant that has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in the tropical regions of Central and South America. The bioactive constituents of Uragoga smithii include alkaloids, glycosides, and saponins, which are responsible for the plant's medicinal properties. 

The most common herbal preparations made with Uragoga smithii include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat a variety of health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
